business_definition: Unique ID of the new claim intake case.
validation_or_usage_notes: Pattern CLM-######. Use as the case key across future datasets.

business_definition: Date the claim intake record was submitted/created.
validation_or_usage_notes: Must be on or before the dataset snapshot date.

business_definition: Channel through which the claim was submitted.
Policy reference supplied by the claimant.
validation_or_usage_notes: May be blank; agent must use Policy Eligibility Lookup rather than assume a match.

business_definition: Customer reference supplied by the claimant.
validation_or_usage_notes: Synthetic identifier only; may conflict with policy in deliberate review scenarios.

business_definition: Device identifier supplied in the claim intake.
validation_or_usage_notes: May be blank or intentionally mismatch the covered-device record in test scenarios.

business_definition: Incident date stated in the structured intake form.
validation_or_usage_notes: Blank means the agent should request clarification; narrative may also be contradictory in review scenarios.

business_definition: Claim type selected in the intake form.
validation_or_usage_notes: SCREEN_DAMAGE | ACCIDENTAL_DAMAGE | LIQUID_DAMAGE | MECHANICAL_BREAKDOWN | THEFT | LOSS | UNSPECIFIED.
REPAIR | REPLACEMENT | UNSPECIFIED. It does not by itself decide eligibility.
Pattern EBND-######. The actual evidence status is not held in this dataset.
Synthetic English text. Use this for claim understanding; do not treat it as authoritative over policy/rules.

business_definition: Lifecycle status of the intake record.
Date at which this synthetic dataset was frozen.
